forecasts.reasoning: Muqtada al-Sadr announced on March 27, 2025 that he would boycott the 2025 Iraqi parliamentary elections. He then reaffirmed this position on April 18, 2025 with firm statements like 'I have not participated, and I will never participate alongside the corrupt and the enemies of the people.' While some sources close to al-Sadr suggested in April 2025 that the boycott is 'not final' and he may be 'considering an indirect path to participate,' no formal announcement or reversal had been made by early May 2025. Given that: (1) al-Sadr has twice publicly committed to the boycott with strong anti-corruption framing, (2) only 2 months remain between the knowledge cutoff (May 3, 2025) and the resolution deadline (July 1, 2025), and (3) al-Sadr has historically used strategic absences to maintain political leverage but is currently branded as the 'Patriotic Shiite Movement' with no electoral registration confirmed, the probability of a public announcement reversing the boycott before July 1 is relatively low.
